footer.tagline: Create professional, customizable resumes in minutes. Choose from 25 templates and export to PDF, DOCX, and more.

faq.title: Questions? We've Got Answers
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about using ResumeCraft — from privacy to exporting and everything in between.

questions.q: Where is my resume data stored?
questions.a: Entirely in your browser's local storage. We don't have servers, accounts, or databases. Your data never leaves your device unless you choose to export or share it.

Yes. ResumeCraft is a Progressive Web App (PWA). Once you've visited the site, it works fully offline — including creating, editing, and exporting resumes. You can even install it as an app on your phone or desktop.

questions.q: How do I export my resume as a PDF?
questions.a: Open the resume you want to export, click the Export button in the top bar, and select PDF. The conversion happens entirely in your browser — nothing is sent to a server. You can also export as DOCX, JSON, HTML, or plain text from the same menu.

Completely. Since everything lives in your browser, there's no way for us — or anyone else — to access your resume data. The only exception is if you choose to share a generated link or file yourself.

questions.q: How many resumes can I create?
questions.a: As many as you want. There are no limits, no paid tiers, and no subscription. All 25+ templates are free to use on every single resume.

questions.q: Can I use the templates for commercial purposes?
questions.a: Absolutely. Any resume you create with ResumeCraft is yours to use however you like — job applications, freelance proposals, portfolio showcases, you name it.

questions.q: What happens if I clear my browser data?
questions.a: Your resumes will be deleted. We recommend exporting important resumes as PDF or DOCX backups. You can also export as JSON, which you can re-import later to continue editing.

questions.q: Is there a way to recover deleted resumes?
questions.a: Unfortunately, no. Because data is stored locally, there's no undo once it's deleted. This is why we recommend regular backups via JSON export.

questions.q: Will my data be used to train AI models?
questions.a: Never. We don't have access to your data to begin with. Your resume content stays on your device and is never transmitted anywhere.

Yes, always. ResumeCraft is open source under the MIT License. There are no paid plans, no hidden features behind a paywall, and no ads. If you find it useful, the best way to support it is by starring the repo on GitHub or contributing.

questions.q: How do I report a bug or request a feature?
questions.a: Open an issue on our GitHub repository. We review every submission and typically respond within a few days.

privacy.title: Your Data Stays Yours
privacy.subtitle: We believe in radical transparency. Here's exactly how ResumeCraft handles your data.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: ResumeCraft is designed with your privacy first. Because your resume data is stored entirely in your browser's local storage, we never have access to the personal information you enter.

sections.title: How We Use Your Information
sections.content: Since we don't collect your resume data, our use of information is minimal and transparent.

sections.title: Local Storage & Your Control
sections.content: ResumeCraft uses your browser's localStorage API to store your resume data.

sections.title: Third-Party Services
sections.content: ResumeCraft integrates with the following third-party services: GitHub Pages / Vercel for hosting, and export libraries that run entirely in your browser.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: Because your resume data never reaches our servers, the primary security risk is on your own device.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: Depending on your jurisdiction (e.g., GDPR in the EU, CCPA in California), you may have rights including access, deletion, and data portability.

sections.title: Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: Have questions about this privacy policy or how we handle your data? Reach out to us via GitHub Issues.

terms.title: Clear, Fair, Transparent
terms.subtitle: These terms govern your use of ResumeCraft. We've kept them straightforward.

sections.title: Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By using ResumeCraft, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. The Service is provided as a free, open-source tool without warranty of any kind.

sections.title: Description of Service
sections.content: ResumeCraft is a static web application for creating and managing professional resumes. The Service runs entirely in your browser.

sections.title: User Responsibilities
sections.content: As a user, you agree to use the Service for lawful purposes only and maintain your own backups of important resume data.

sections.title: Intellectual Property
sections.content: You retain full ownership of all information you enter. The ResumeCraft application is open source under the MIT License.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
sections.content: ResumeCraft is provided free of charge without any guarantees. We are not liable for any loss of data.

sections.title: Third-Party Links
sections.content: The Service may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for their content or privacy practices.

sections.title: Changes to Terms
sections.content: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes will be posted with an updated date.

sections.title: Governing Law
sections.content: These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: Questions about these terms? Reach out through our GitHub repository.

accessibility.title: Resume Building for Everyone
accessibility.subtitle: We believe creating a professional resume should not be a barrier for anyone.

commitment.title: Our Commitment
commitment.description: We follow the WCAG 2.2 AA standard as our benchmark and are continuously improving.

items.title: Keyboard Navigation
items.description: Every interactive element is reachable and operable via keyboard. Tab through forms, use Enter/Space to toggle, and never get trapped in focus loops.

items.title: Visual Design
items.description: High contrast ratios, clear typography, and a dark mode option reduce eye strain. All text meets or exceeds WCAG AA contrast requirements.

items.title: Reduced Motion
items.description: Respecting prefers-reduced-motion at the system level. Animations are decorative, never essential to understanding or using the app.

items.title: Screen Reader Support
items.description: Semantic HTML structure, ARIA labels on interactive elements, and meaningful alt text on all images. Forms announce errors clearly.

standards.title: Standards & Testing
standards.description: We test against the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 at Level AA. Our testing process includes automated audits, manual keyboard testing, screen reader testing, and focus management verification.

limitations.title: Known Limitations
limitations.subtitle: We're transparent about where we're still improving.

items.title: PDF Export Accessibility
items.description: Exported PDFs currently use visual rendering via html2canvas, which produces flattened images rather than tagged accessible PDFs. We recommend using DOCX export for screen-reader-friendly documents.

items.title: Template Color Customization
items.description: Some templates offer limited color contrast options. We are working toward ensuring all template presets meet AA contrast standards by default.

feedback.title: Help Us Improve
feedback.description: Accessibility is a journey, not a destination. If you encounter a barrier using ResumeCraft, please let us know.
